content.problemStatement:
You are given a dictionary **student_grades** where the keys are the names of students and the values are their corresponding grades.

Take a string input - the name of a student and print the grade of the student if his/her name is present in given dictionary else print **Not Found**.
constraints: Each name consists of only alphabetic characters and has length between 1 and 20
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 15), the number of queries. Each of the next T lines contains a single string – the name of a student (no leading/trailing spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each query, output the grade of the student if the name exists in the predefined dictionary, otherwise output "Not Found". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: "Bob" exists in the dictionary with grade 72, so 72 is printed.
"Elena" is not a key in the predefined dictionary, therefore the program prints "Not Found".
Alice and John are present with grades 85 and 45 respectively; Zoe is absent, so "Not Found" is printed for the third query.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
15
Alice
Bob
Charlie
David
Eva

72

Not Found
Not Found
Not Found

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
Not Found
Not Found
Not Found

solution.approach: Store the six given name‑grade pairs in a hash map (or parallel arrays). For each query read the name and look it up in the map. If the name is present, output the associated grade; otherwise output "Not Found". This gives O(1) average time per query.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each of the T queries is answered in constant average time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – the map contains only six fixed entries.
